label: Toe tendinitis without rupture or diabetic-foot red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated toe tendinitis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed toe tendinitis. We did not find tendon rupture, fracture, infection, poor blood flow, diabetic-foot red flags, or another toe emergency today.
home: Rest the toe from activity that makes pain worse.
Use ice, heat, gentle motion, support, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Return to normal activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, numbness, or weakness.
return: A pop, new deformity, inability to walk, blue or pale toe, black skin, or severe worsening pain.
New deformity, severe worsening pain, inability to use the area, or a new injury.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, podiatry, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated toe tendinitis.

No tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Tendon rupture, fracture or dislocation concern, open wound, infection concern, diabetic foot, PVD, neuropathy, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed podiatry/orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and separation from rupture, infection, trauma, or neurovascular compromise. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated toe tendinitis with rupture, fracture, infection, diabetic-foot, and neurovascular red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated toe tendinitis

label: Scapular strain without fracture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit an uncomplicated scapular strain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a scapular strain. We did not find fracture, dislocation, tendon rupture, nerve or blood-flow problems, chest emergency, or another emergency today.
home: Rest the shoulder blade area from activity that makes pain worse.
Use ice, heat, gentle motion, support, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Return to normal activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, numbness, or weakness.
return: New arm numbness, weakness, blue or pale hand, chest pain, trouble breathing, or severe worsening pain.
New deformity, severe worsening pain, inability to use the area, or a new injury.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated scapular strain.
No fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection, cardiopulmonary red flags, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, infection concern, chest pain, respiratory distress, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports sprain/strain care and escalation for severe pain, numbness, weakness, color change, or function loss. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated scapular strain with fracture, neurovascular, tendon, and cardiopulmonary red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated scapular strain

label: Localized adhesive rash without infection or anaphylaxis
summary: Your symptoms fit a localized skin reaction from adhesive.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a localized adhesive skin reaction. We did not find anaphylaxis, mucosal involvement, skin infection, or another rash emergency today.
home: Avoid the suspected trigger if it is known.
Wash gently with mild soap and water, and avoid scrubbing the rash.
Use cool compresses, moisturizers, or coverings only as your clinician recommended.
meds: Use creams, ointments, itch medicine, or allergy medicine only if prescribed or recommended.
Do not use leftover steroid, antibiotic, or allergy medicine unless your clinician told you to.
return: Trouble breathing, throat tightness, swelling of the lips or tongue, fainting, or a bodywide reaction.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, pus, red streaks, rapidly worsening rash, skin peeling, or mouth sores.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized adhesive skin reaction.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, skin infection, severe medication reaction, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin infection, rapidly progressive rash, burn or chemical exposure, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant or allergic exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed topical or allergy treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, infectious, ocular, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized adhesive skin reaction with anaphylaxis, infection, mucosal, and severe-rash red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of localized adhesive skin reaction
no infection, mucosal, or severe-rash red flags
